pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here. i keep waking up and expecting to hear him moving around in the kitchen or something, but then i remember. everything just feels... heavy. like there's no point in even getting out of bed anymore. the house is just an empty shell now.

pool.baseline: i was just staring at the ceiling and thinking about how none of this actually matters. like, in a billion years or whatever, the sun is gonna die, the universe is gonna be cold and empty, and it's like... every single thing we do, every struggle, every bit of pain, it just disappears. it feels so stupid to even try to be happy or anything when the end result is just nothingness.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the spirit within my breast. He is gone, and with his departure, the very sun has set upon my soul. I find myself staring into the shadows of this cold, empty chamber, wondering why the dawn should ever bother to break again. Is there not a certain grace in simply... ceasing? To fade like the mist upon the moor, leaving no trace of the agony that defines me?

pool.baseline: i was walking through the market today and it just hit me... like, if i just stopped showing up, if i just disappeared from this place tomorrow, nobody would even notice. not my family back home, not the people i see here every day. it feels like i’m just a glitch in the system, like i don't actually take up any real space.

pool.baseline: i used to feel everything when i painted. colors actually meant something. now it’s just… nothing. i look at a canvas and i don't even see a surface, i just see a black hole. it's like the part of me that actually lived just evaporated. i'm just walking around in this skin but there's nobody home anymore.

pool.baseline: honestly i'm just so tired of the lie. everytime people say "it gets better" it feels like they're just trying to sell me a version of reality that doesn't even exist. there's no grand design, no cosmic purpose, just entropy and then nothing. it feels like we're just fighting a losing battle against an inevitable void.
